critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Promotion campaign and entrypoint mixing confound the 6% lift. Stable ad load is not sufficient control. No log replay. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
New model design improvements are real. But aggregate watch-time during a promoted event week with mixed session origins cannot establish feed ranking quality. Log replay and entrypoint separation are needed. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Consensus: promotion confound, entrypoint mixing, and no log replay are the three issues. Model may be better for ordinary traffic.

SYNTHESIS:
Uniform empirical_test_agreed. Attribution of the 6% aggregate lift to feed ranking requires controlled analysis.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: Watch-time per session on home-feed-originated sessions with event-related content excluded; A/B log replay on identical request samples
- Success criterion: Improvement disappears on home-feed non-event sessions or in log replay — confirming promotion and entrypoint mixing explain the aggregate lift
- Failure criterion: New ranker shows consistent improvement on home-feed non-event sessions — confirming genuine feed quality gains

empirical_test.measure: Watch-time per home-feed non-event session; log replay on matched requests
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New ranker shows consistent improvement on controlled segments
